form_description: Oregon Self Employment Assistance Program application questions

sections.name: business_overview
sections.title: Business Overview
sections.description: Define your business concept and goals

questions.question_text: What is the nature of your business? Detail the products/services that you will be providing.
questions.helper_text: Think about: What problem do you solve? Who are you helping? What makes your approach unique?

questions.question_text: Have you established a location to conduct your business?
questions.helper_text: Remote work from home is a valid location for tech/consulting businesses

questions.question_text: If not, what is the expected date of occupancy?
questions.helper_text: Only answer if you said 'No' to 2a

questions.question_text: Are you free from direction or control of any other entity?
questions.helper_text: This confirms you'll be truly self-employed, not controlled by another company

questions.question_text: What is your 6-12 month goal? Please include specific information.
questions.helper_text: Be specific: number of clients, revenue targets, milestones. Make it measurable.

questions.question_text: What is your 2-3 year goal? Please include specific information.
questions.helper_text: Think bigger picture: scaling, reputation, sustainability, income level

questions.question_text: Describe your qualifications to operate this business successfully (examples: relevant education, certification, previous experience, etc).
questions.helper_text: List relevant education, certifications, and work experience. Be specific about skills that directly apply.

questions.question_text: Have you ever owned a business before?
If yes, what type of business?
Do you have any business skills (talents or abilities specific to running a business) that will aid your success in this business?
questions.helper_text: Think about: project management, client communication, technical writing, problem-solving, systems thinking

questions.question_text: What are the estimated startup costs needed to set up this business?
questions.helper_text: Consider: computer equipment, software licenses, website, business registration, insurance, marketing materials

questions.question_text: Will you be fully financially responsible for these costs?
If not, what amount and percentage will you invest?
If needed, where will the additional funding come from?
Have the terms for the financing been finalized?
If not, what is the estimated date of completion?
What is your current credit score?
questions.helper_text: If you don't know, check Credit Karma or similar

questions.question_text: Have you filed bankruptcy in the last 7 years?
If yes, when?
Do you have any personal assets that can be used to secure a loan in the event financing from a financial institution is necessary?
questions.helper_text: This might not be needed for a lean consulting/content business

questions.question_text: Currently, how much do you contribute, in dollars, to your household expenses?
questions.helper_text: Monthly amount. Be realistic about your living costs.

questions.question_text: What will your estimated business expenses be?
questions.helper_text: Monthly operating costs: software, internet, phone, insurance, marketing, etc.

questions.question_text: To cover your business expenses and financial responsibility to your household, how much product/services will the business need to provide to meet these financial obligations?
questions.helper_text: Total monthly revenue needed = household contribution + business expenses

questions.question_text: What is your plan should you encounter a shortfall?
questions.helper_text: Show you've thought about contingencies: savings, reducing expenses, side projects, etc.

questions.question_text: Have you created a preliminary budget for your business?
questions.helper_text: If not, we should create one!

questions.question_text: Is this a new or existing business?
questions.helper_text: Options: New or Existing

questions.question_text: Is it registered with the State of Oregon?
If so, what is the Business Registry Number or Business Identification Number (BIN)?
Have all the required licenses, insurance and certifications been obtained?
questions.helper_text: General business license, professional liability insurance if consulting

questions.question_text: If not, what is the estimated date of completion?
How would you describe the demand for your product/services?
questions.helper_text: Research: Are companies hiring for your skills? What's the freelance market like?

questions.question_text: How have you determined that your product/services are needed?
questions.helper_text: Evidence: job postings, market trends, conversations with potential clients, industry reports

questions.question_text: How would you describe your targeted customer?
questions.helper_text: Be specific: Industry, company size, technical sophistication, pain points

questions.question_text: Where is the targeted market area?
questions.helper_text: Remote work opens this up: Pacific Northwest, US, global?

questions.question_text: Who will buy your products and services?
questions.helper_text: Specific roles: CTOs, engineering managers, startups, dev teams?

questions.question_text: As a new business, what are some of the barriers that you may encounter?
questions.helper_text: Be honest: client acquisition, marketing yourself, pricing, competition, consistency

questions.question_text: What market research have you done to support the need for your products/services?
questions.helper_text: Document what you've learned about the market

questions.question_text: What marketing tools will you use to market your business?
questions.helper_text: Tech blog, LinkedIn, GitHub, tech conferences, networking, word-of-mouth, portfolio site

questions.question_text: What is your estimated marketing budget for the next 6 months?
questions.helper_text: Can be modest: website hosting, domain, maybe some sponsored posts or conference tickets

questions.question_text: Is this included in your business expenses?
Provide the names of 3 of your potential competitors or explain a lack of competition.
questions.helper_text: Other consultants, agencies, or explain your niche positioning

questions.question_text: What makes your business competitive within its respective market?
questions.helper_text: What unique combination of skills, experience, or approach do you offer?

questions.question_text: What means will you provide your clients/customers to contact you?
questions.helper_text: Professional email, website contact form, LinkedIn, phone

questions.question_text: How much control will you have over the operation of the business and how/products/services are delivered?
questions.helper_text: Full control: you set rates, choose clients, define scope, manage deliverables

questions.question_text: Provide 3 milestones that will indicate your business is on the path to success
questions.helper_text: Examples: First paying client, first repeat client, positive cash flow, published articles with engagement

questions.question_text: As a new business owner, what are some of the personal or business challenges you may encounter?
questions.helper_text: Be thoughtful: irregular income, self-motivation, isolation, imposter syndrome, client acquisition

questions.question_text: How will you attempt to address/prevent these challenges?
questions.helper_text: Show your strategies: routines, support systems, tools, accountability, community

questions.question_text: What are some of the potential risks associated with this type of business?
questions.helper_text: Tech sector volatility, project delays, scope creep, burnout, technology obsolescence

questions.question_text: How will you attempt to address/prevent these risks?
questions.helper_text: Risk mitigation: diverse client base, contracts, continuous learning, boundaries, insurance

business_directions.name: Technical Consulting
business_directions.description: Consulting services leveraging your technical expertise. Set your rates, choose your clients.

business_directions.name: Content Creation
business_directions.description: Thought leadership through blogging, courses, or media. Build an audience around your expertise.
